---
title: "When our own push protocol leaked conflict markers"
description: "A real multi-writer collision: the semantic resolver reported failure and the wrapper committed the conflict markers anyway, because the resolver's exit code was ignored."
date: "2026-07-19"
categories: [field-notes, tooling]
draft: true
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status"}
**Incident + fix.** A real bug we hit and how it was resolved.
:::

**What happened.** The nine CI scenarios never caught it because every test resolver succeeds; the fix checks the exit code and greps for markers before committing, plus a new failing-resolver scenario.

**Why it matters.** A real multi-writer collision: the semantic resolver reported failure and the wrapper committed the conflict markers anyway, because the resolver's exit code was ignored.

**Details.** Tracked upstream as issue #94 on the llm-wiki-memory-template. Our instance is still on the pre-fix copy; the fix is to pull, not to re-derive.
